content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 4, 2), (3, 8, 2), (1, 2, 5), (6, 7, 5), (3, 5, 8), (0, 1, 10), (2, 3, 14), (7, 9, 15), (1, 9, 17), (5, 7, 17), (2, 6, 18), (0, 4, 19), (7, 8, 21), (0, 9, 24), (3, 6, 24)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 3 exactly at time 24.

First, we will go through each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (3, 4, 2) has node 3 and time 2. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (3, 8, 2) has node 3 and time 2. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (1, 2, 5) has node 1 and time 5 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 7, 5) has node 6 and time 5 (not 2). -> Add node 7.
* Edge (3, 5, 8) has node 3 and time 8. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (0, 1, 10) has node 0 and time 10 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 3, 14) has node 2 and time 14 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (7, 9, 15) has node 7 and time 15 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 9, 17) has node 1 and time 17 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 7, 17) has node 5 and time 17 (not 2). -> Add node 7.
* Edge (2, 6, 18) has node 2 and time 18 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 4, 19) has node 0 and time 19 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (7, 8, 21) has node 7 and time 21 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 9, 24) has node 0 and time 24 (not 2). -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 6, 24) has node 3 and time 24. -> Add node 6.

The nodes matched are 4, 8, and 7.

Answer: [4, 8, 7]
